*highlighted section*  
**1. Living Room LED Light Output Function and Energy Consumption**

To maintain a constant illumination level of 500 lumens in the living room, we need to calculate the LED light output function $ L(t) $, which adjusts based on the natural light function $ N(t) $. The LED light output is the difference between the desired total illumination and the natural light:

$$ L(t) = 500 - N(t) = 500 - (300 + 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)) $$

Simplifying the equation:

$$ L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) $$

This function represents the LED light output needed to maintain exactly 500 lumens throughout the day.

Next, we calculate the total energy consumption of the LEDs over a 24-hour period. The energy consumption is given by the integral of the LED light output function multiplied by the power consumption rate (0.1 watts per lumen-hour):

$$ \text{Energy Consumption} = \int_0^{24} L(t) \times 0.1 \, dt $$

$$ \text{Energy Consumption} = 0.1 \int_0^{24} (200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right)) \, dt $$

$$ \text{Energy Consumption} = 0.1 \left[ 200t + 2400\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \right]_0^{24} $$

$$ \text{Energy Consumption} = 0.1 \left[ (200 \times 24 + 2400\cos(2\pi)) - (200 \times 0 + 2400\cos(0)) \right] $$

$$ \text{Energy Consumption} = 0.1 \left[ 4800 + 2400 - 2400 \right] $$

$$ \text{Energy Consumption} = 0.1 \times 4800 = 480 \text{ watt-hours} $$
